justification: All pass criteria met: 12-month growth projection calculated (~9x, 446K orders/day); database identified as primary bottleneck; 85% disk utilization flagged as critical near-term emergency with ~2 month timeline; index fix on customer_id identified as highest ROI first step; clear phased plan (indexes/tuning → read replicas/cache → partitioning/archival); sharding explicitly deferred in favor of read replicas for 4-engineer team; trade-offs include replication lag and cache invalidation complexity.

observations.text: Growth projection table with monthly breakdowns is exceptionally detailed, showing exact orders/day, DB size, CPU, and disk utilization at each milestone
Disk emergency correctly identified as critical risk requiring day-1 action before any engineering work
Full table scan root cause identified and index fix with CONCURRENTLY flag recommended
Three-phase plan is well-structured with realistic effort estimates (engineer-weeks) appropriate for a 4-person team
Sharding explicitly deferred: 'Read replica over sharding' with rationale that it's overkill for 12-month horizon
Trade-offs table covers replication lag, cache invalidation, eventual consistency, and operational complexity
Practical pitfalls section adds significant implementation value (CONCURRENTLY for index, PgBouncer necessity, async replication)
Confidence assessment with explicit assumptions and low-confidence areas shows intellectual honesty
DB size projection at month 12 shows 1.7TB rather than ~3.6TB (400GB × 9); appears to account for base size differently but the discrepancy is not explained
Flexibility and rigidity points provide useful architectural guidance for future evolution

agent_output_excerpt:
# Capacity Analysis & Scaling Plan

## Growth Projections

Before diving into architecture, let me establish the numbers driving every decision:

| Month | Orders/Day | DB Size (est.) | DB CPU (est.) | Disk Util. |
|-------|-----------|----------------|---------------|------------|
| 0 (now) | 50,000 | 400 GB | 70% | 85% |
| 2 | 72,000 | 480 GB | ~100% ⛔ | ~100% ⛔ |
| 4 | 103,000 | 590 GB | saturated | overflow |
| 6 | 149,000 | 740 GB | saturated | overflow |
| 9 | 258,000 | 1.1 TB | saturated
